_readme: Content OS · Trend source registry. ONE file, many source objects. Each object's 'type' is api | rss | scrape. Add a platform = add one object to 'sources'. NO workflow code for api/rss. Edit in VS Code (validates JSON). Committed to Git, so NEVER put API keys here — use 'auth_ref' to name an n8n credential; the runtime swaps {auth} with the real secret. This file knows NOTHING about pages — page↔source wiring lives in the Supabase 'page_trend_sources' table. Placeholders like {region} in 'url' are filled per-pull from each page's subscription. A different response SHAPE or TIMEFRAME = its own source object, not a placeholder.
_field_map_help: field_map keys are OUR standard TrendDetected fields; values are the path inside the platform's response for one item. Target keys: topic (required), description, category, keywords, region, country, language, volume_value, volume_unit, rank, image_url, related, external_id, url, detected_at. 'volume_unit' is a literal — wrap literals as {"const": "searches"}. Paths use dot notation; for RSS the item is one <item> with tag names as keys (namespaced tags use ht: form).
_timeframe_help: timeframe is carried on each source (now | daily | weekly | monthly | yearly). It flows into the TrendDetected event (for the daily Analytics report to group by) AND joins the dedup key, so 'google_trends_daily Dune 3' and 'google_trends_monthly Dune 3' are two different trends. A platform only gets entries for windows it actually supports — no timeframe knob, just separate named sources.

sources.name: google_trends_daily
sources.notes: WORKING. Google Trends daily trending searches RSS. No key. {region}=geo (TW,US,JP...).

sources.name: google_trends_weekly
sources.url: CONFIRM_ENDPOINT — Google has no clean weekly RSS; wire to a 7-day Trends source before enabling
sources.notes: SCAFFOLD (disabled). Structure + timeframe ready; needs a real 7-day endpoint confirmed before enabling. Do NOT enable with the daily URL or it silently returns daily data.

sources.name: google_trends_monthly
sources.url: CONFIRM_ENDPOINT — Google has no clean monthly RSS; wire to a 30-day Trends source before enabling
sources.notes: SCAFFOLD (disabled). Structure + timeframe ready; needs a real 30-day endpoint confirmed before enabling.

sources.name: youtube
sources.notes: WORKING once key set. YouTube Data API v3 mostPopular (always 'now' — no timeframe siblings). part=snippet,statistics for viewCount(volume)+tags(keywords)+thumbnail. {auth} swapped from n8n credential. rank assigned by list position at parse time.

sources.name: dcard_tw
sources.notes: SCRAPE type (disabled). The only type that may need code in the Scrape skeleton. field_map points at the extracted item's data.

sources.name: youtube_film
sources.notes: WORKING. YouTube Film & Animation category (videoCategoryId=1) = movie/film trends. Region per subscription (US, TW).

_examples_for_later._comment: Copy into 'sources' to onboard a platform. Loader ignores keys starting with _.

youtube_shorts_DIFFERENT_SHAPE.name: youtube_shorts
youtube_shorts_DIFFERENT_SHAPE.notes: Own entry only because a different shape needs its own field_map. Same-shape variations are page subscriptions, NOT new entries.

generic_rss.name: some_news_rss
generic_rss.notes: Any RSS feed = change url + field_map. Usually ZERO code.
